steps.title: Resuspend Vero E6 cells to a...
steps.instruction: Resuspend Vero E6 cells to a concentration of 30,000 cells per 50 µL (6.0x10^5^/mL) in growth media. Record passage number. Transport cells from clean tissue culture BSC to viral BSC.

steps.title: Seed 50 µL of cells to...
steps.instruction: Seed 50 µL of cells to each well of a white-bottomed tissue culture coated 96-well plate.

steps.title: Dilute virus of interest to MOI=0
steps.instruction: Dilute virus of interest to MOI=0.01 in 50 µL/well. Add viral antigen to columns 2-12. D

steps.title: Add 50 µL blank media to column 1
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL blank media to column 1.

steps.title: Place at 37°C for 24 hours
steps.instruction: Place at 37°C for 24 hours.

steps.title: After incubation, remove supernatant via aspiration...
steps.instruction: After incubation, remove supernatant via aspiration and wash twice with PBS.

steps.title: Add 200 µL of 4% paraformaldehyde...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL of 4% paraformaldehyde to wells while working in the biosafety cabinet.

steps.title: Wrap plates in plastic film, spray...
steps.instruction: Wrap plates in plastic film, spray down with 70% EtOH and remove from biosafety cabinet to fume hood.

steps.title: Let fix for 20 minutes at...
steps.instruction: Let fix for 20 minutes at room temperature, then remove fixative and dispose into appropriate waste container.

steps.title: Rinse wells 3X with PBS and dump in waste container
steps.instruction: Rinse wells 3X with PBS and dump in waste container. Remove from fume hood. Remaining steps can be performed on the bench top.

steps.title: Add 100 µL of 0
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL of 0.5% Triton X-100 in PBS to each well to permeabilize the monolayer and incubate at RT for 10-15 minutes.

steps.title: Rinse wells 3X with PBS and dump down the drain
steps.instruction: Rinse wells 3X with PBS and dump down the drain.

steps.title: Add 100-200 µL of blocking agent...
steps.instruction: Add 100-200 µL of blocking agent and place at 4°C overnight or at minimum for 1 hour.

steps.title: Thaw sera samples on ice and...
steps.instruction: Thaw sera samples on ice and dilute 10 µL heat-inactivated sera into 990 µL diluent to prep a 1:100 stock of diluted sera. This can be saved for future repeats of the assay.

steps.title: Prep your sample plate
steps.instruction: Prep your sample plate. To an uncoated, 96-well assay plate, add 75 µL antibody dilution buffer to columns 2-12.

steps.title: Into column 1 of the sample...
steps.instruction: Into column 1 of the sample plate, add 150 µL of diluted sample to yield a 1:100 starting dilution.

steps.title: Perform 1:2 dilutions, transferring 75 µL...
steps.instruction: Perform 1:2 dilutions, transferring 75 µL of sample to next well in series, changing tips each time.

steps.title: Bring blocked plate from cold room and dump block
steps.instruction: Bring blocked plate from cold room and dump block. Tap dry on blotting paper.

steps.title: Add 50 µL of dilute sera samples to plate
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL of dilute sera samples to plate. Can start from most dilute to least dilute and use same tips on multichannel.

steps.title: Incubate at RT for 4 hours...
steps.instruction: Incubate at RT for 4 hours or 37°C for 1 hour with gentle rocking.

steps.title: Dump samples and rinse 3X with...
steps.instruction: Dump samples and rinse 3X with PBS-T, making sure to let wash set for 3 minutes between each rinse to reduce background signal.

steps.title: Add 50 µL 2° antibody diluted...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL 2° antibody diluted in antibody dilution buffer to each well. Incubate at RT for 1 hour.

steps.title: Dump samples and rinse 3X with...
steps.instruction: Dump samples and rinse 3X with PBS-T, making sure to let wash set for 3 minutes between each rinse to reduce background signal.

steps.title: Tap plates dry and allow to air-dry for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Tap plates dry and allow to air-dry for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Add 100 uL peroxidase substrate and...
steps.instruction: Add 100 uL peroxidase substrate and incubate at room temperature in dark for 10 minutes, or until signal develops.

steps.title: Endpoint titer is last well with...
steps.instruction: Endpoint titer is last well with visible foci and is reported as the 1:X inverse dilution.

steps.title: At this point, the plate can...
steps.instruction: At this point, the plate can be stored for later use. If storing, add 100 uL of PBS to each well and wrap with plastic film or parafilm and store at 4°C.

steps.title: Blocking agent and length will impact...
steps.instruction: Blocking agent and length will impact the sensitivity, specificity and background of the assay and should be optimized for each experimental sample type.

steps.title: Example plate layout with antigen negative...
steps.instruction: Example plate layout with antigen negative wells in grey and antigen positive wells in purple.

steps.title: Expected results of the CBA, showing...
steps.instruction: Expected results of the CBA, showing clear foci in columns 2-12, absence of foci in the antigen null column 1, and clear positive control staining (Row G, LCMV 1-1-3 antibody) and negative control staining (Row H, null serum). Endpoint titer corresponds to column with last positive signal, in this case is A=7, B=4, C=8, D=5, E=5, F=8, G=0. Expected results of the CBA. Plate imaged on the CTL ImmunoSpot, but can be read by eye.
